parinibbāyino.meaning: one who attains final liberation; those who reach final nibbāna
parinibbāyino.gram: adjective, genitive singular or nominative plural of parinibbāyī

pariniṭṭhitamesānā.meaning: seeking what is completely finished; the search now wholly concluded
pariniṭṭhitamesānā.gram: sandhi compound: past participle + present participle (pariniṭṭhitaṁ + esānā)

paripāko.meaning: ripeness; maturity; (of the faculties) decay through ageing
breakdown: To cook, ripen; paripāka is full ripening, maturity — and, of the body, breaking down with age.

paripūrakārī.meaning: acting in full; one who practises completely, fulfilling (the training)

paripūressanti.meaning: they will fulfil; they will complete, bring to perfection
paripūressanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural future of paripūreti (causative of paripūrati, pari + √pūr)
breakdown: To fill; paripūreti means to fulfil, complete.
Third person plural future ending: "they will".

parisaṅkanti.meaning: they suspect; they are apprehensive about
parisaṅkanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of parisaṅkati (pari + √saṅk)

parisaṅkāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, dative/ablative singular of parisaṅkā

parisodhesuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of parisodheti (pari + √sudh, causative)
breakdown: To be pure; the causative parisodheti means to clean, purify.

parisuddhaṁ.gram: past participle of parisujjhati (pari + √sudh), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: To be pure; parisuddha means thoroughly purified.

parisuddhaṁ, paripuṇṇam.meaning: completely pure, completely full; pure and perfect all round
parisuddhaṁ, paripuṇṇam.summary: This is a stock pair describing the holy life: all-round pure + all-round complete.
parisuddhaṁ, paripuṇṇam.gram: phrase: two past participles, accusative/neuter singular

parisuddhā.gram: past participle of parisujjhati, plural or feminine form

breakdown: To sit; parisā is those "seated around", an assembly.

parisānuggahena.meaning: out of consideration for the assembly; through help or patronage of the company
breakdown: Help, patronage, kindness (anu + √gah). (The long ā at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)

paritta.gram: neuter noun (protection); also past participle/adjective "small" (pa + √ric + ta)
breakdown: Protection, safeguard; the protective recitations (as in the Āṭānāṭiya and other paritta texts).
Small, limited, trifling (past participle of pa + √ric "to leave, empty").
paritta.summary: Two distinct words share this form: "protection" (as in protective verses) and "small, insignificant".

breakdown: To leave, empty; paritta means left small, limited, trifling.

parittāni.gram: adjective, neuter nominative/accusative plural of paritta

parivajjayeyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of parivajjayati (pari + √vajj)
breakdown: To avoid; parivajjeti means to shun, keep clear of.

parivuto.gram: past participle of parivāreti (pari + √var), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: To cover, enclose; parivuta means surrounded.

parivīmaṁsanapariyosāne.meaning: at the conclusion of the thorough investigation

parivīmaṁsati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, desiderative formation (pari + vīmaṁsati, √man)

pariyantavatiṁ.meaning: having limits; well-defined, having a purpose (said of speech)
pariyantavatiṁ.gram: adjective, feminine accusative singular of pariyantavant
breakdown: Limit, boundary, end (pari + anta, with euphonic y).

pariyesati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present (pari + √es, with euphonic y)
breakdown: To seek; pariyesati means to search all round for.

pariyodapana.goenka: The word appears in the celebrated summary of the teaching of all the Buddhas (Dhammapada 183, cited in the tradition's material): sacittapariyodapanaṁ, the complete purification of one's own mind — alongside avoiding all evil and cultivating wholesomeness. Purification of mind is precisely what the practice of meditation is for: not a doctrine to accept, but a cleansing to be carried out within oneself.

pariyodapeyya.meaning: should completely cleanse; would purify thoroughly
pariyodapeyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of pariyodapeti (pari + ava + √dā, causative)

pariyodāpenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of pariyodāpeti (pari + ava + √dā, causative)

pariyodātā.meaning: cleansed all round; pure, bright, white
pariyodātā.gram: past participle of pariyodāyati, plural or feminine form

pariyogāḷhadhammo.meaning: one who has dived into the Dhamma; having plunged into the teaching
breakdown: Dived into, plunged into (pari + ava + √gāh + ta, with euphonic y).
pariyogāḷhadhammo.goenka: The phrase describes the disciple who has not merely heard the Dhamma but plunged into it: the stock passage runs diṭṭhadhammo pattadhammo viditadhammo pariyogāḷhadhammo — having seen, attained, known and dived into the Dhamma. The sequence expresses the tradition's experiential standard: the teaching is penetrated by direct experience, beyond doubt, not held as belief.

pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ.meaning: beneficial in the end; beautiful in its conclusion
pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ.goenka: Part of the stock description of the Dhamma well proclaimed: ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ — beneficial in the beginning, beneficial in the middle, beneficial in the end. In the tradition's presentation, the beauty of the beginning is sīla, of the middle samādhi, and of the end paññā ripening in the fruit of nibbāna.

pariyādinnacitto.meaning: with mind overpowered; whose mind is exhausted, having lost control of the mind
breakdown: Taken over completely, exhausted, overpowered (past participle of pariyādiyati).

pariyādāya.meaning: taking hold of completely; overpowering, exhausting
pariyādāya.gram: gerund of pariyādiyati (pari + √ādā, with euphonic y)
breakdown: Completely, all round. (Followed by euphonic y.)
To take; pariyādiyati means to take over wholly, overpower.

pariyāpannā.gram: past participle of pariyāpajjati (pari + ā + √pad), plural or feminine form
breakdown: To get into; pariyāpanna means fallen within, included.

pariyāya.meaning: turn, course; teaching, exposition; cause, reason; way, method (literally "going round")
breakdown: To go; pariyāya is the "going round" — a turn or succession, and by extension a mode of teaching, a figure, a reason.

pariyāyadassāvī.meaning: seeing (only) an exposition; one who realizes by way of the instruction

pariyāyavacanaṁ.meaning: speech in due order; expression by way of succession; synonymous expression

pariyāye.meaning: in due turn; in succession, at the (proper) course
pariyāye.gram: masculine noun, locative singular of pariyāya

pariyāyena.meaning: by this mode of reasoning; by way of (this) method or figure
pariyāyena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular of pariyāya

pariññeyyaṁ.meaning: to be completely comprehended; should be fully understood
pariññeyyaṁ.gram: future passive participle of parijānāti (pari + √ñā), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Completely. (The doubled ññ arises by assimilation with the root.)
To know; parijānāti means to know fully, comprehend.
pariññeyyaṁ.goenka: The word states the task of the First Noble Truth as the tradition presents it: dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ pariññeyyaṁ — the Noble Truth of Suffering has to be completely comprehended. Each truth has its own task (to be fully understood, eliminated, realized, developed), and comprehension here is experiential: dukkha is to be explored within one's own mind and body, not merely accepted as a proposition.

pariññātaṁ.gram: past participle of parijānāti (pari + √ñā), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: To know; pariññāta means fully comprehended.

paro.meaning: another; the other, one (person as opposed to another)
paro.gram: pronoun, masculine nominative singular of para

parā.meaning: aside; back, opposed to (directional prefix); others (plural pronoun)
parā.gram: prefix; also pronoun, plural form of para

parābhavo.gram: masculine abstract noun, nominative singular (from parābhavati, parā + √bhū)
breakdown: Prefix: away, back, to one's loss.
To be; parābhava is going to ruin, downfall.

parābhisajjanī.meaning: cursing others; scolding, offensive towards others
breakdown: Other, others. (The long ā at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)

parāgata.meaning: reached the other side; gone beyond

parāmāsā.meaning: attachment to; being under the influence of; misapprehension, wrong grasp
parāmāsā.gram: masculine noun, ablative singular or plural form of parāmāsa (parā + √mas)
breakdown: To touch; parāmāsa is "touching wrongly" — clinging to, misapprehending (as in sīlabbataparāmāsa, attachment to rites and rituals).
Injuring, hurting (upa + √han). (The long ū at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)

parūpaghātī.meaning: one who injures others; hurting another
breakdown: Injuring, one who hurts (upa + √han + ī). (The long ū at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)

parūparodhavirahitaṁ.meaning: free from obstructing others; exempt from harming or hindering another
breakdown: Obstructing, hindering, harming (upa + √rudh). (The long ū at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)
Bereft of, free from, exempt from (vi + √rah + ita).

pasannena.meaning: pure; purified, clear, serene (instrumental: with a pure mind)
pasannena.gram: past participle of pasīdati (pa + √sad), instrumental singular
breakdown: To settle; pasanna means settled clear, purified, serene; also full of confidence.
pasannena.goenka: The word appears in the second opening verse of the Dhammapada, cited in the tradition's material: manasā ce pasannena bhāsati vā karoti vā — if one speaks or acts with a pure, serene mind, happiness follows like a never-departing shadow. It is the counterpart of paduṭṭhena (with a corrupted mind) in the first verse: the quality of mind behind an action determines its fruit.
